articles.title: How to Choose the Right Startup Funding Path
articles.summary: An overview of bootstrapping, grants, angels, and venture capital for first-time founders.
articles.content: Choosing how to fund your startup is one of the earliest strategic decisions you’ll make. This guide breaks down the trade-offs between bootstrapping, equity-free grants, angel investment, and venture capital. We’ll look at how much capital each path typically offers, what expectations come with the money, and how to align your funding strategy with your long-term goals.

articles.title: Equity-Free Grants vs Equity Investment: A Practical Guide
articles.summary: Compare equity-free grants and equity investment programmes so you can pick the right funding track.
articles.content: Not all funding is created equal. Equity-free grants give you capital without dilution but are often milestone-driven and competitive. Equity investment programmes exchange capital and support for a stake in your company. In this guide, we break down typical cheque sizes, equity percentages, control implications, and how to assess the true cost of capital.

articles.title: MVP Validation Checklist: Test Before You Build
articles.summary: A step-by-step checklist to validate your startup idea with an MVP before investing heavily in product development.
articles.content: Most startups fail because they build something nobody wants. Use this checklist to design a minimum viable product that actually tests demand. You’ll learn how to define your riskiest assumptions, select lean experiments, recruit test users, and measure traction using simple metrics like activation, retention, and willingness to pay.

articles.title: Customer Interviews that Actually Validate Demand
articles.summary: Learn how to run customer interviews that surface real problems and buying intent instead of polite feedback.
articles.content: Good customer interviews focus on past behavior, not future promises. This article walks through how to recruit the right participants, craft non-leading questions, and analyze interview notes for patterns. We include scripts you can copy, plus red flags that indicate you’re collecting compliments instead of validation.

articles.title: Legal Basics for Founders: Incorporation, IP, and Contracts
articles.summary: A plain-language guide to the key legal decisions every founder should make in the first year.
articles.content: Legal work doesn’t have to be intimidating or expensive. In this guide, we cover when and where to incorporate, how to split equity among co-founders, the basics of intellectual property protection, and the essential contracts you should have in place with employees, contractors, and early customers.

articles.title: Choosing the Right Company Structure in the US
articles.summary: Compare LLCs, C-corps, and sole proprietorships for US-based startups.
articles.content: Your company structure affects taxes, fundraising options, and founder liability. We compare LLCs, C-corps, S-corps, and sole proprietorships with examples of when each makes sense, especially for high-growth startups raising outside capital.

articles.title: Lean Marketing Playbook for Early-Stage Startups
articles.summary: How to acquire your first 100 customers with a tiny budget and lots of iteration.
articles.content: Instead of big campaigns, early-stage marketing is about fast experiments. This playbook outlines how to define a target persona, select 2–3 traction channels, set up basic analytics, and run weekly marketing sprints that compound learning over time.

articles.title: 5 Financial Metrics Pre-Seed Investors Actually Care About
articles.summary: Cut through the noise and focus on the financial metrics that matter for early-stage fundraising.
articles.content: You don’t need a full CFO dashboard at pre-seed, but you do need to understand runway, burn rate, unit economics, and early signs of traction. This article covers how to calculate and present these metrics in a way that builds investor confidence.

articles.title: 7 Real MVP Experiments from Successful Startups
articles.summary: Concrete examples of MVP experiments you can model for your own idea.
articles.content: From concierge MVPs to landing-page pre-sales, we break down seven real-world MVP experiments, including what was tested, how success was measured, and what each founder learned.

articles.title: Time Management for Founders with a Day Job
articles.summary: Strategies to make consistent progress on your startup in 5–10 hours per week.
articles.content: If you’re building your startup on the side, you need ruthless prioritization. This guide covers weekly planning, batching work, and eliminating distractions so you can ship meaningful progress in limited time.

articles.title: Understanding Your First Term Sheet
articles.summary: A non-lawyer’s guide to the key terms in early-stage investment deals.
articles.content: We explain valuation, liquidation preferences, pro rata rights, and other clauses you’ll see in a standard term sheet, with founder-friendly examples and negotiation tips.

articles.title: Building a Community Around Your Startup
articles.summary: How to cultivate an engaged user community from day one.

articles.title: Old Legal Checklist for Founders (Archived)
articles.summary: Superseded legal checklist kept for archive purposes.

articles.title: MVP vs Prototype: What’s the Difference?
articles.summary: Clarify the difference between prototypes, MVPs, and full products.
articles.content: Understanding the difference between prototypes and MVPs helps you invest the right amount of effort at each stage. We define each concept and share examples.

mentors.bio: Ben is a fintech product manager turned founder with experience building B2B payment products in Europe and Latin America.
Ella has 7+ years of experience leading product and growth at consumer fintech startups in the US and UK.
Cara is a former investment banker and CFO who now mentors fintech founders on fundraising and financial strategy.
SaaS founder with two exits, focused on B2B sales and pricing strategy.
Social impact entrepreneur helping mission-driven founders design sustainable business models.
E-commerce strategist specializing in DTC brands, conversion optimization, and retention.
Healthtech founder with experience navigating regulations and hospital sales cycles.
Growth marketer helping early-stage teams set up performance marketing and analytics.
Startup lawyer advising on incorporation, fundraising, and IP.

quiz_questions.question_text: What best describes your current startup stage?
How much time can you realistically commit to your startup each week?
What is your primary goal for the next 3 months?
How many people are currently on your founding team (including you)?
Which of the following best describes your current traction?
In which area do you feel you need the most support right now?
What is your current average monthly revenue?
How much external capital do you need over the next 12 months?
How comfortable are you with giving up equity in your company in exchange for funding and support?
Which of the following are your top priorities for seeking funding? (Select all that apply.)
Where do most of your potential customers spend their time online? (Select all that apply.)
What is your typical deal size or average customer lifetime value?
Have you already incorporated a legal entity for your startup?
Which of the following legal documents do you currently have in place? (Select all that apply.)

quiz_options.option_text: Prototype or MVP built, not yet launched
Launched with early users or revenue
Validating my idea / finding problemsolution fit
Still at idea stage, no users yet
Some user signups or pilot users, no revenue
